panels.title: Platformers — things to investigate
panels.content:
## Game state and structure

Platformers typically have a clear separation between the overworld or menu, active stage, pause, and game over states. These may live in a single state index, separate flags, or a combination. The demo flag may be part of the same index.

## Health and lives

Platformers handle player health in many different ways. Some games use a discrete integer for hit points. Some use a power-up state that doubles as health (taking damage removes the power-up). Some use only a damage flag with no persistent health value. Some use lives as the only resource, with a single hit ending the current life. Investigate what changes when the player takes damage and work from there.

## Score and BCD

Older arcade-style platformers often store score in BCD (binary-coded decimal) rather than a standard integer. If a score that should read as 1000 shows a different value in the memory viewer, BCD is worth investigating.

## Collectibles and flags

Collectibles gathered within a level and permanent collectibles across the whole game may be tracked differently. Per-level counts often reset on exit. Permanent collection state is often a bitmask — several items packed into one byte. Watch for values that change by powers of 2 rather than by 1.

## Position

Player X and Y coordinates may be split across two bytes each or stored as larger values. Walking in one direction and watching for a consistently increasing or decreasing value is the starting point.

panels.title: Protection
panels.infobox: Identify these before writing achievement logic.

entry_Protection.name: Demo mode
entry_Protection.tNote: Check for a specific value that is set or cleared only on the main menu or when demo mode initiates. The demo flag may be a standalone address or part of a larger game state index.

entry_Protection.name: Save protection
entry_Protection.tNote: Find values that match a loading state or last loaded in-game state so that you can confirm the player is in-game for at least one frame by checking Delta = in-game.

entry_Protection.name: Cheat protection
entry_Protection.tNote: Investigate old guides or walkthroughs for possible cheats. If they offer an advantage, block them. Look for on/off flags, unnatural resource increases during a menu or pause scenario.

entry_Protection.name: Invincibility cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, damageless and lives-based achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: Infinite lives cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, achievements tied to surviving without extra lives must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: All levels unlocked cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, progression achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: All power-ups cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, power-up collection achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: Infinite time cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this exists, timed stage achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: Skip stage / level select
entry_Protection.tNote: If stage skipping is accessible as a cheat, progression achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: 2-player mode active
entry_Protection.tNote: Co-op or versus mode. Investigate whether this needs to be excluded from solo achievements.

entry_Protection.name: Continue used flag
entry_Protection.tNote: Whether a continue has been used. Useful for no-continue challenge protection.

panels.title: Progression
panels.infobox: Values that track forward movement through worlds, stages, and story.

entry_Progression.name: Game state / screen index
entry_Progression.tNote: A value that changes between the title screen, overworld, active stage, pause, cutscene, game over, and credits.

entry_Progression.name: World ID
entry_Progression.tNote: Which world or region the player is currently in.

entry_Progression.name: Stage / level ID
entry_Progression.tNote: Which level within the current world is loaded.

entry_Progression.name: Difficulty setting
entry_Progression.tNote: The selected difficulty. May be an index, separate flags, or encoded into the save data.

entry_Progression.name: Stage completion flag
entry_Progression.tNote: A value that changes when the current stage is cleared.

entry_Progression.name: World completion flag
entry_Progression.tNote: A value that changes when all stages in a world are cleared.

entry_Progression.name: Boss defeat flag — boss 1
entry_Progression.tNote: Whether boss 1 has been permanently defeated. May be an individual flag or a bit in a shared byte.

entry_Progression.name: Boss defeat flag — boss 2
entry_Progression.tNote: Whether boss 2 has been permanently defeated.

entry_Progression.name: Boss defeat flag — boss 3
entry_Progression.tNote: Whether boss 3 has been permanently defeated.

entry_Progression.name: Boss defeat flag — boss 4
entry_Progression.tNote: Whether boss 4 has been permanently defeated.

entry_Progression.name: Boss defeat flags (combined)
entry_Progression.tNote: All boss states may be packed into a single byte rather than individual addresses.

entry_Progression.name: Story / event flags
entry_Progression.tNote: Flags for cutscenes, NPC triggers, or story beats.

entry_Progression.name: Area unlock flags
entry_Progression.tNote: Whether specific locked areas or paths have been opened.

entry_Progression.name: Character select unlock
entry_Progression.tNote: Whether alternate playable characters are available.

entry_Progression.name: Game completion flag
entry_Progression.tNote: A value set when the credits roll or the final screen is reached.

entry_Progression.name: NG+ / second loop flag
entry_Progression.tNote: Whether the current run is a New Game+ or second loop state.

entry_Progression.name: Total stages cleared (count)
entry_Progression.tNote: A running total of stages completed, if one exists.

entry_Progression.name: Clears per character
entry_Progression.tNote: If the game tracks completion separately per playable character, where that data lives.

entry_Progression.name: Ending variant
entry_Progression.tNote: Which ending was reached, for games with multiple possible conclusions.

entry_Progression.name: Cutscene / dialogue seen
entry_Progression.tNote: Whether a specific cutscene or NPC conversation has been viewed.

panels.title: Collections
panels.infobox: Things the player has permanently collected or unlocked.

entry_Collection.name: Coins / collectibles (current level)
entry_Collection.tNote: Count of items gathered in the active level. Investigate whether this resets on exit or carries over.

entry_Collection.name: Coins / collectibles (total)
entry_Collection.tNote: Global running total across all levels, if one exists.

entry_Collection.name: Collectible flags — world 1
entry_Collection.tNote: Which collectibles in world 1 have been permanently gathered. May be a bitmask.

entry_Collection.name: Collectible flags — world 2
entry_Collection.tNote: World 2. Compare address offset from world 1 to determine entry size.

entry_Collection.name: Collectible flags — world 3
entry_Collection.tNote: World 3. The same offset pattern from world 2 should apply.

entry_Collection.name: Secret area found flags
entry_Collection.tNote: Which hidden or optional areas have been entered.

entry_Collection.name: Secondary collectible (stars, gems)
entry_Collection.tNote: If the game has a secondary collectible type, where its count lives.

entry_Collection.name: Power-up inventory
entry_Collection.tNote: Stored power-ups held between stages, if the game has this system.

entry_Collection.name: Character unlock flags
entry_Collection.tNote: Which playable characters have been unlocked.

entry_Collection.name: Costume / skin unlock flags
entry_Collection.tNote: Alternate visual unlocks, if they exist.

entry_Collection.name: Item chest or container opened flags
entry_Collection.tNote: Which treasure chests or item boxes have been permanently opened.

entry_Collection.name: In-game currency / points
entry_Collection.tNote: Spendable currency if the game has a shop system.

entry_Collection.name: Upgrade flags
entry_Collection.tNote: Which permanent upgrades or skills have been purchased or learned.

entry_Collection.name: Extra lives (current)
entry_Collection.tNote: How many lives the player currently has in reserve.

entry_Collection.name: Bonus / gallery content unlocks
entry_Collection.tNote: Music, artwork, or extra modes unlocked through collection milestones.

panels.title: Challenges
panels.infobox: Values for achievements with pass/fail conditions.

entry_Challenge.name: Player HP (current)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Current health value. The data type and range depend on the game's health system.

entry_Challenge.name: Player HP (max)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Maximum health. May increase with upgrades. Useful for ratio-based logic.

entry_Challenge.name: Damage / hit flag
entry_Challenge.tNote: A value that changes when the player takes damage.

entry_Challenge.name: Invincibility / i-frames
entry_Challenge.tNote: A short-lived value active during post-hit recovery. The window may be only a few frames.

entry_Challenge.name: Power-up state (active)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Which power-up is currently active. May also serve as a health indicator in games where taking damage removes the power-up.

entry_Challenge.name: Score
entry_Challenge.tNote: Current score. May be a standard integer, BCD, or a scaled value.

entry_Challenge.name: Combo counter
entry_Challenge.tNote: Consecutive hits or enemy defeats without interruption.

entry_Challenge.name: Stage timer
entry_Challenge.tNote: May be a float, frame counter, integer in hex, BCD, centiseconds in a single address, or split across minutes, seconds, and centiseconds. Search for a changing value during an active timed stage.

entry_Challenge.name: Player X position
entry_Challenge.tNote: Horizontal coordinate in the level. May be split across multiple bytes.

entry_Challenge.name: Player Y position
entry_Challenge.tNote: Vertical coordinate. May be adjacent to X or at a separate address.

entry_Challenge.name: Checkpoint reached
entry_Challenge.tNote: Which checkpoint is currently active as the respawn point.

entry_Challenge.name: Boss HP (current)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Only populated while a boss encounter is active.

entry_Challenge.name: Boss HP (max)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Useful for phase-threshold logic. May be adjacent to boss current HP.

entry_Challenge.name: Enemy kill counter
entry_Challenge.tNote: A count of enemies defeated. May be global or per-type.

entry_Challenge.name: Player lives (current)
entry_Challenge.tNote: How many lives are currently in reserve.

entry_Challenge.name: Death counter (session)
entry_Challenge.tNote: How many times the player has died in the current run.

entry_Challenge.name: Death counter (total)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Lifetime death count, if stored persistently.

entry_Challenge.name: Clears per character — char 1
entry_Challenge.tNote: How many completions character 1 has, if tracked separately.

entry_Challenge.name: Clears per character — char 2
entry_Challenge.tNote: How many completions character 2 has.

entry_Challenge.name: Clears per character — char 3
entry_Challenge.tNote: Continue for each additional playable character.

entry_Challenge.name: Jump state flag
entry_Challenge.tNote: Whether the player is currently airborne.

entry_Challenge.name: Speed / velocity
entry_Challenge.tNote: Current movement speed. Useful for speed-based conditions.
